title: senna artemisioides subsp. hamersleyensis
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. hamersleyensis, also known as Hamersley's senna, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family, native to Western Australia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative effects due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the bowels and promote bowel movements. The health benefits of Hamersley's senna include treating constipation, diarrhea, and digestive issues, as well as its potential use in managing menstrual cramps and other gynecological issues. In traditional herbal medicine, Hamersley's senna is prepared by drying the leaves and pods, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for use as a natural remedy.
